subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2025, World Bank, ADB, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Indonesian official (BPS, Bank Indonesia) + regional independent press (Jakarta Post, Tempo, Nikkei Asia, East Asia Forum).
Politics: a Prabowo government since Oct 2024 with a broad coalition (stable mandate) against 2025 austerity protests and democratic-backsliding/military-role concerns.

Standout drags: weak rule of law and endemic corruption, severe seismic/flood disaster exposure, and a sinking capital. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone; Bahasa-Indonesia primary press read only indirectly.

economic.notes: Low debt under a legal ceiling, a credible central bank, and low inflation, with rising state intervention in resource exports the watch-item.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~40% GDP under a 60% legal ceiling, deficit ~2.9% near the 3% legal limit; a high interest bill and new Prabowo spending (a free-meals program) erode credibility. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Bank Indonesia is a credible inflation-targeter (2.5 plus or minus 1%) on a managed float; 2025 rupiah pressure and fiscal-pressure-on-the-central-bank concerns temper it. (Bank Indonesia)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The rupiah is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and within the 2.5 plus or minus 1% band. (Bank Indonesia)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalised, with IMF-noted resilience. (IMF Art IV 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A large commodity base (top nickel/coal/palm) plus manufacturing, but downstreaming policy and a 2026 move to route key exports via state firms raise distortion and paradox-of-plenty risk. (CSIS; Lowy)

institutional.notes: Usable official statistics, undercut by endemic corruption, a weakened anti-graft body, and declining press freedom.

rule_of_law.notes: Endemic corruption, the anti-graft KPK gutted since the 2019 law, and declining judicial independence (TI CPI ~34). (Transparency International 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: BPS is usable and credible; 2025 budget cuts are a watch-item. (IMF uses BPS data)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Mixed: patronage and active-duty generals placed in civilian posts. (HRW; East Asia Forum)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 127/180 ('difficult'), with a collapsing safety indicator amid police/military intimidation. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A favourable demographic dividend, against declining civil liberties and a backsliding trend under a new government.

political_stability.notes: A Prabowo government (since Oct 2024) with a large coalition and stable mandate, against 2025 austerity protests, a military-law revision, and backsliding. (East Asia Forum; HRW)

civil_liberties.notes: Declining: blasphemy/defamation laws, 2025 protest crackdowns, and digital intimidation. (HRW; RSF)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A second-home/investor KITAS plus retirement routes (Bali popular); lawful but bureaucratic. (immigration regulations)

social_cohesion.notes: Pluralist (Pancasila) but with rising religious conservatism and Papua/regional tensions. (V-Dem; regional press)

demographics.notes: A young, large workforce in an open dividend window. (BPS; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned ASEAN leader hedging the major powers, with low direct conflict exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned ASEAN leadership, hedging the US and China, having joined BRICS in 2025 while pursuing OECD accession - predictable non-alignment rather than bloc reliability. (The Diplomat; CSIS)

conflict_involvement.notes: Natuna/South China Sea friction with China, with low direct conflict. (CSIS; East Asia Forum)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Broadly open, though 2026 state-firm export-routing and downstreaming bans signal rising intervention. (IMF; regional press)

physical_practical.notes: Improving infrastructure and near-universal health coverage, against extreme seismic/flood exposure and a sinking capital.

climate.notes: A vast archipelago - sea-level rise (sinking Jakarta), coastal/delta exposure, heat; improving but limited readiness. (WB CCDR; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: BPJS/JKN near-universal coverage (~250m) but uneven in quality and low in per-capita spend. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (the IKN new capital, toll roads) but congested and uneven. (Trade.gov)

disaster_insurance.notes: Very low catastrophe-insurance penetration against extreme seismic/flood exposure. (GFDRR)

summary: A large, demographically favourable economy with prudent macro management - low debt under a legal ceiling, a credible central bank, and low inflation - and a non-aligned ASEAN-leadership posture. The drags are weak rule of law and endemic corruption, declining civil liberties and press freedom under a new government, rising state intervention in resource exports, and extreme natural-disaster exposure capped by very thin catastrophe insurance. Near-universal health coverage and improving infrastructure are real positives.
